description_en: The Utsubushi forest, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1982-02-03.
The Shirasawa irrigation channel (yosui), designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1982-02-03.

name_en: Shoenzuka Mound and the Solitary Pine
description_en: The Shoenzuka mound and its solitary pine tree, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1982-02-03.

name_en: Tomb of the Martyr Yamaguchi Rokurouemon
description_en: The tomb of the peasant martyr (gimin) Yamaguchi Rokurouemon, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1960-05-20.
Kuryu Hachimangu shrine, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1960-05-20.
The Umakakure cedar tree, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1976-03-30.

name_en: Koyamaki Umbrella Pine of Shogakuji Temple
description_en: The koyamaki umbrella pine of Shogakuji temple, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1976-03-30.
The weeping cherry tree (shidarezakura) of Kamikogabu, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1996-02-28.
The zelkova tree (keyaki) of Kurumaya, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1996-02-28.
The rock-splitting cherry tree (ishiwarizakura), designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1996-02-28.
The great cedar tree (osugi) of Untokuji temple, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1996-02-28.
The maple tree (kaede) of the Yakushi hall, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1996-02-28.

name_en: Precinct of the Hirakawa Furutakian Fudoson
description_en: The temple precinct of the Hirakawa Furutakian Fudoson, designated as a place of scenic beauty. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture.
The great kaya (Japanese nutmeg) tree of Furudera, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1991-10-14.
The cherry tree of Yamazuma-u, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Numata City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2007-02-02.

A poem monument (Sango eika-hi), classified as calligraphy under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Meiwa, Gunma Prefecture. Located in the garden of the Morijiri residence, Minami-oshima.

A stone tablet (itabi) depicting the Amida triad, classified as a stone object under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Meiwa, Gunma Prefecture. Located on the Akino residence grounds, Yajima.

A stone tablet (itabi) of the Showa (medieval Shōwa era) period, classified as a stone object under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Meiwa, Gunma Prefecture. Located on the Aoki residence grounds, Tajima.

name_en: Sutra Mound with Stone Sutra Pagoda
description_en: A sutra mound (kyozuka) with an attached stone sutra pagoda, designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Meiwa, Gunma Prefecture. Located within the precinct of Niisato Tenmangu shrine.
The torii gate of Sugawara Shrine, designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Meiwa, Gunma Prefecture. Located at Sugawara Shrine, Suka.
The Eguro ancient burial mound (kofun), designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Meiwa, Gunma Prefecture. Located in Eguro.

name_en: Site of the Kawamata Incident Clash
description_en: The site where the clash of the Kawamata Incident took place, designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Meiwa, Gunma Prefecture, on 1999-12-28. Located near the Kawamata Kamijuku bridge, Kawamata.
The Nishimoku barrier station (sekisho), designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-10-23.

name_en: Nakaosaka Iron Mine and Ironworks Site
description_en: The site of the Nakaosaka iron mine and ironworks, designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-10-23.

name_en: Large Cast-Iron Brazier Made at the Nakaosaka Iron Mine
description_en: A large cast-iron brazier (hibachi) produced at the Nakaosaka iron mine, designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1985-07-17.
The architectural carvings of Suwa Shrine, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1979-06-29.
The great zelkova tree (keyaki) of Suwa Shrine, designated as a natural monument. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1979-06-29.
A stone tablet (itabi) at Anrakuji, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-10-23.
The Fudoson statue of Ashonen, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-10-23.
The temple bell (bonsho) of Seisenji, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1979-06-29.
The Hokyoin stone pagoda (hokyointo) of Seisenji, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1994-03-22.
The five-ring stone pagoda (gorinto) of Ogawara, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-10-23.
The Hokyoin stone pagoda (hokyointo) of Akatani, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-10-23.

name_romaji: Kūkyo Shōnin Hitsu Hannya Shingyō no Hi
name_en: Heart Sutra Monument Brushed by the Monk Kukyo
description_en: A stone monument inscribed with the Heart Sutra in the hand of the monk Kukyo, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1984-09-11.
The site of the Shimonita battle, designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1982-02-02.

name_en: Seated Statue of Shaka Nyorai of Chorakuji
description_en: A seated statue of Shaka Nyorai (Shakyamuni) at Chorakuji, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-10-23.
A group of one hundred Koshin stone monuments at Iseyama, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1984-09-11.
A group of one hundred Koshin stone monuments at Abuta, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1988-03-22.
A group of one hundred Koshin stone monuments at Shimizusawa, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-10-23.
The eighty-eight sacred pilgrimage sites (reijo) of Hattoya, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1979-06-29.
